commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a three-attempt retry/backoff helper for `getTailnetHostname` status JSON reads, focused regression coverage, and a local proof script.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +24, Other +33. Total +85 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this read-only review. The source path is clear: current main enables Tailscale Serve and then performs a one-shot `tailscale status --json` hostname lookup without retry.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied live Tailscale proof and normal maintainer review, not a narrow automation-repair defect.

target.title: fix(tailscale): retry status json after serve startup
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the transient `tailscale status --json` startup race in Tailscale Serve mode.

- add a narrow same-binary retry helper around the status JSON read used by `getTailnetHostname`
- keep the change scoped to serve-startup hostname discovery; no config or funnel behavior changes
- add focused regression coverage for a first-call status failure that succeeds on retry
- add a runnable proof script that exercises the real `getTailnetHostname` path with an injected transient failure

Closes #42798.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro/tailscale-status-json-retry-live-proof.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `CODEX_HOME=/private/tmp/codex-autoreview-home HOME=/Users/tuaran .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt 'Review the local fix for openclaw/openclaw#42798. The patch should only add a narrow retry/backoff around tailscale status JSON reads used for serve startup hostname discovery, without expanding config surface or changing funnel behavior.'` *(blocked by this automation environment's restricted network before a review verdict could be returned)*

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway Tailscale Serve startup can query `tailscale status --json` too quickly after `tailscale serve --bg --yes`, producing a transient first-call failure even though the same binary succeeds on the next attempt.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with Node `v24.11.0`, running the real `getTailnetHostname` production code path from this branch with an injected first-call `status --json` failure.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx scripts/repro/tailscale-status-json-retry-live-proof.mjs`

Evidence after fix: `status calls: 2` and `host: proof-host.tailnet.ts.net`

Observed result after fix: The first simulated `tailscale status --json` failure is retried on the same binary, and the second call resolves `proof-host.tailnet.ts.net` instead of surfacing a transient startup error.

What was not tested: A live Homebrew Tailscale daemon restart under real host load; funnel mode and non-serve paths were intentionally left unchanged.
target.author: tuaran
